text:
Reflection summary: The original keep_watch stance for NVIDIA post-earnings momentum setup appears directionally reasonable, but the lesson should be reused cautiously and with scenario-fit checks.

Execution summary:
- entry_date: 2025-05-26
- exit_date: 2025-06-02
- entry_price: 135.5
- exit_price: 142.3

Outcome metrics:
- realized_pnl_pct: 5.02
- max_drawdown_pct: -1.8

What worked:
- The original decision incorporated portfolio context rather than treating the setup in isolation.
- Historical decision-memory cases were available to frame the current setup against prior analogs.
- The final recommendation stayed bounded enough to align with the later realized outcome.

What failed or was underweighted:
- No major reasoning failure was confirmed, but the review should avoid over-generalizing from one case.

Reusable lessons:
- Reuse historical cases only when the current setup matches on scenario fit, not just superficial similarity.
- A correct outcome still warrants bounded reuse because a single successful case does not prove the broader rule.
- Portfolio constraints should remain part of postmortem review because they materially shape the quality of the recommendation.

Future adjustments:
- Capture the realized outcome and lessons as a reusable postmortem memory before future similar setups are reviewed.
- Require future reflections to compare the final recommendation against both supporting and disconfirming historical cases.
- Keep future reuse measured instead of upgrading the pattern into a blanket rule after one success.

metadata.title: NVDA NVIDIA post-earnings momentum setup Postmortem
metadata.subject: NVIDIA post-earnings momentum setup
